description: Fix route naming inconsistency (6.1) and frontend-backend disconnections (6.8): Replace /api/super-admin/ routes with redirects to canonical /api/superadmin/ path, add audit-logs route to superadmin, fix settings/audit page to use tenant audit logs directly (which it already does via server-side query - no API call needed), create /api/tenant/products route for the products page, and create /api/tenant/segments route for dynamic segments.
steps: Step 1: Replace app/api/super-admin/tenants/route.ts content with a redirect/proxy that forwards all methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) to /api/superadmin/tenants. Use NextResponse.redirect or import and re-export the handlers from the canonical route.

Step 2: Replace app/api/super-admin/audit-logs/route.ts with a proper superadmin audit-logs endpoint under the canonical path. Create app/api/superadmin/audit-logs/route.ts with the same logic currently in app/api/super-admin/audit-logs/route.ts. Then replace app/api/super-admin/audit-logs/route.ts with a redirect to the canonical path.

Step 3: Create app/api/tenant/products/route.ts with GET (list products for tenant with search/filter), POST (create product using createProductSchema from lib/api/schemas.ts). Follow the pattern in app/api/tenant/services/route.ts. The products table is already defined in drizzle/schema/crm.ts.

Step 4: Create app/api/tenant/products/[id]/route.ts with GET (single product), PATCH (update product using updateProductSchema), DELETE (soft delete by setting deletedAt). Follow pattern from app/api/tenant/services/[id]/route.ts.

Step 5: Create app/api/tenant/segments/route.ts with GET (list segments for tenant) and POST (create segment). Use the segments and segmentMembers tables from drizzle/schema/segments.ts. Add createSegmentSchema and updateSegmentSchema to lib/api/schemas.ts.
Step 6: Create app/api/tenant/segments/[id]/route.ts with GET, PATCH, DELETE for individual segment management.

Step 7: Create app/api/tenant/service-categories/route.ts with GET and POST for managing service categories. Use serviceCategories table from drizzle/schema/billing.ts. Add createServiceCategorySchema to lib/api/schemas.ts.

Step 8: Create app/api/tenant/price-books/route.ts with GET and POST for managing price books. Use priceBooks and priceBookEntries tables from drizzle/schema/crm.ts. Add createPriceBookSchema to lib/api/schemas.ts.
Step 9: Create app/api/tenant/price-books/[id]/route.ts with GET, PATCH, DELETE for individual price book management.
acceptance_criteria: app/api/superadmin/audit-logs/route.ts exists with proper GET handler
app/api/tenant/products/route.ts exists with GET and POST handlers using products table
app/api/tenant/products/[id]/route.ts exists with GET, PATCH, DELETE
app/api/tenant/segments/route.ts exists with GET and POST handlers using segments table
app/api/tenant/segments/[id]/route.ts exists with GET, PATCH, DELETE
app/api/tenant/price-books/route.ts exists with GET and POST
app/api/tenant/price-books/[id]/route.ts exists with GET, PATCH, DELETE
New zod schemas (createSegmentSchema, createServiceCategorySchema, createPriceBookSchema) added to lib/api/schemas.ts
verification: Verify files exist: ls app/api/superadmin/audit-logs/route.ts app/api/tenant/products/route.ts app/api/tenant/segments/route.ts app/api/tenant/service-categories/route.ts app/api/tenant/price-books/route.ts
findings: TypeScript compilation passes cleanly (npx tsc --noEmit). The z.record() call in Zod requires two arguments (key and value schema) in this project's version - use z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) pattern. The serviceCategories table uses utils.audit() which includes createdBy, updatedBy, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt, deletedBy fields. The priceBooks table also uses utils.audit(). The segments table uses utils.audit() as well.
